data.body:
User feedback from /taxonomize review (2026-04-16): 'apply' is overloaded across 7+ subjects (events, git, plan edits, listing filters, doctor fixes, snapshot restore, step transitions) and does not add meaning in any of them. Replace each call site with a verb that names the subject. Candidates from TAXONOMY.md:

- apply_filters -> filter_list (src/listing.rs:15)
- apply_fixes -> run_doctor_fixes (src/doctor_fix.rs:32)
- apply_latest_snapshots -> restore_from_snapshots (src/snapshots.rs:141)
- apply_rehydrate_event -> fold_rehydrate_event or rehydrate_with_event (src/app/rehydrate/apply_event.rs:12)
- apply_state_transition_locked -> perform_state_transition_locked (src/app/state_ops.rs:113)
- apply_step_transition -> record_step_transition (src/app/helpers.rs:380)
- apply_to_head -> integrate_worktree_to_head or similar (src/sync/apply.rs:57)

Acceptance: no pub fn or pub(crate) fn in src/ (excluding tests) named apply_* unless the project decides a specific remaining use of 'apply' is semantically correct. Update ARCHITECTURE.md and TAXONOMY.md accordingly.
data.title: Rename all uses of 'apply' to subject-specific verbs
